### Post by Plasma_NZ on 2013-05-11
Ok...

x2 displays - one samsung 22Inch monitor and one 46" LG tv on hdmi

Tv is connected via HDMI - running on 1080p resolution.....  I getting tearing only on the top of any video i play...

I run seperate xsessions and have compiz on the monitor, configured and set just for that monitor....

My hardware is a GTX 660Ti OC2 - more than enough grunt to do what im asking flawlessly.

Here is my xorg.conf setup..

```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 310.44  (buildmeister@swio-display-x86-rhel47-07)  Wed Mar 27 15:56:09 PDT 2013


Section "ServerLayout"

EndSection

EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device1"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce GTX 660 Ti"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          1
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"

    BoardName      "GeForce GTX 660 Ti"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          0
EndSection

Section "Screen"

# Removed Option "metamodes" "DFP: 1680x1050 +0+0"
# Removed Option "metamodes" "DFP-0: 1680x1050 +0+0"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "Stereo" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "DFP-0: 1680x1050_60 +0+0"

    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"

# Removed Option "metamodes" "CRT: 1920x1080 +0+0; CRT: nvidia-auto-select +0+0; CRT: 1680x1050 +0+0; CRT: 1600x900 +0+0; CRT: 1440x900 +0+0; CRT: 1400x1050 +0+0; CRT: 1360x768 +0+0; CRT: 1280x1024 +0+0; CRT: 1280x800 +0+0; CRT: 1280x720 +0+0; CRT: 1152x864 +0+0; CRT: 1024x768 +0+0; CRT: 800x600 +0+0; CRT: 640x480 +0+0"
```

any idea's how i can stop this..? works fine when in windows doing basically the same, zero tearing there... im assuming my refresh for the TV is perhaps out slightly.?
